title: Other Law Enforcement Resources - Canada.ca
content:
## Other Law Enforcement Resources
Health Canada conducts activities to promote and monitor compliance with regulations made under the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act. In addition to municipal and provincial police forces, the following are the federal, American and international agencies involved in compliance and monitoring activities of controlled substances and precursor chemicals.
These links and documents are provided to help Canadian law enforcement agents and agencies keep abreast of national and international issues related to controlled drugs and substances.
- Canada Border Services Agency
- Controlled Drugs and Substances Act (and associated regulations)
- Food and Drugs Act (and associated regulations)
- Department of Justice Canada Home Page
- Royal Canadian Mounted Police
- Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) The DEA is the American federal enforcement agency that deals specifically with the control of drugs and substances.
- National Institute of Drug Abuse (NIDA) NIDA is an American government agency that supports research on drug abuse and addiction, and ensures the rapid and effective transfer of scientific data to policy makers, drug abuse practitioners, other health care practitioners and the general public.
- United States Department of Justice
- International Narcotics Control Board
- United Nations Drug Control Program
